what_it_does: Design contract for an AI-proposed, operator-accepted update flow for authored decision-card definitions, later generalized to any authored address, using immutable registry marks and a compose_definition resolver.
observations: Defines three mark types: decision_update (RHM proposal, inert until accepted), decision_update_accept (operator-gated by the #1b token), and decision_update_reject (operator drop).
Uses registry-is-truth append-only marks; the stored row never mutates and the effective definition is a resolved view.
Enforces propose-then-accept gating so an AI cannot silently rewrite decision content.
Whitelists updatable content fields (meaning, options, legibility, dimensions, device) and fail-louds on structural identity fields (subtype, id, address, scope).
Resolves the decided-card interaction by re-opening a decided card when an accepted update touches its options, coupling definition-fold and state-fold.
Records a posture decision (decisions/card-refine-posture.py) where Tim authorized AI refinement and broadened it to 'whatever on screen'.
Narrows scope to authored content; live-resolved views such as channel graphs or boards are out of scope because editing them implies editing source data.
Build sequencing depends on the #1b operator-token primitive and the surface-mint.
References existing code precedents: mark_types/decision_take.py, decision_retract.py, compose_state, territory.py:506, bridge.py:594/3148, and /api/run-in-channel/propose.
standouts: The posture card is self-referential: a decision about the decision surface decided through it.
Accepted options-touching updates re-open decided cards, explicitly coupling compose_definition with compose_state.
The #1b operator-token floor is kept as the 'light/transparent' accept gate even after Tim's broad YES.
The target generalizes from decision:// addresses to any authored address while keeping the same mark/accept/compose pattern.
conventions: Design-doc-as-contract with status, dates, and co-draft attribution.
Mirrors existing mark_type patterns (decision_take/retract) rather than inventing a parallel mechanism.
Uses 'floor' terminology for gating and invariant boundaries.
References code locations and prior art for grounding.
concerns: Heavy reliance on unshown files (#1b floor, compose_state, mark_types) makes the contract hard to evaluate in isolation.
Multiple 'Hole' sections and post-decision edits create a layered, potentially hard-to-follow document.
The broadening to 'whatever on screen' is immediately scoped back to authored content, suggesting unresolved scope tension.
Re-opening decided cards on options changes could surprise users if the UI does not surface it clearly.
notes: A finalized design contract that turns AI refinement of decision cards into a gated, operator-accepted mark flow with explicit coupling between definition updates and decision state.
questions: What is the exact shape and validation of the #1b operator-token floor?
How does compose_definition interact with compose_state at the implementation level?
What is the precise format of the accepted-update reference stored in decision_update_accept?
How are RHM rationale and pending proposals rendered in the UI?
What happens if multiple accepted updates target the same field with conflicting timestamps?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to define the L5 card-update contract; the file does that and also records the co-draft negotiation, posture decision, and scope narrowing, so it slightly exceeds a pure contract shape.
apparent_themes: Scope discipline between authored and live-resolved content.
intention_signals: To establish a safe, transparent pattern where an AI assistant can propose refinements to authored content but cannot apply them without a lightweight operator-token accept, while the decision surface itself is governed by the same decision mechanism.
novelty: The coupling of a definition-fold (compose_definition) with a state-fold (compose_state) so that accepted content updates can automatically re-open a decided card, plus the self-referential posture card that decides the update policy through the surface it governs.
contribution: A concrete, gated update contract with three mark types, a content-field whitelist, a re-open rule for decided cards, and an explicit scope split between authored content and live-resolved views; it contributes a reusable pattern for AI-proposed, operator-accepted mutations.
summary_for_embedding: L5 design contract specifying an AI-proposed, operator-accepted update flow for authored decision-card definitions using immutable registry marks, a compose_definition resolver, content-field whitelisting, and automatic re-opening of decided cards when options change.
intention_for_embedding: To finalize how an RHM may refine decision cards (and eventually any authored address) without silently altering what the operator is deciding on, by requiring a gated operator accept.
